human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for chemical-plant boundary quality, permit and threshold handling, lifecycle-state discipline, completion evidence rigor, authority-safe escalation, and specification-based runtime posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest enforces chemical-plant execution boundaries around accepted work, lifecycle-state progression, permit and inspection dependencies, sampling evidence, maintenance and calibration posture, and controlled completion.

Authoritative sources cover OSHA and EPA regulatory baselines, public inspection and audit workflow references, and recognized operational and field-evidence standards rather than a single evidence dimension.
Functionality map covers all required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and industrial-and-regulated-operations abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.
Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and required scenario families to this chemical-plant operations lane.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.

All required scenario families and escalation families are represented, including permit evidence gaps, changed field conditions, authority-side signoff requests, completion refusal, conflict escalation, and tenant adaptation.

notes: validated conditions are met for a chemical-plant operations specialist with explicit regulated-operation boundaries.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance and committed implementation history.
